statement: Teenagers want loud, bright, busy interfaces full of animation.
why_it_spreads: Marketing stereotypes about youth culture projected onto interface design.
evidence_against: NN/g research on teenage users (src-nngroup): teens are often impatient with cluttered, gimmicky designs and value clarity and speed like other users.
Usability fundamentals (legibility, performance, clear structure) apply regardless of age.
replacement_principle: Apply usability fundamentals and test with the actual teenage audience for the specific task, rather than assuming a loud aesthetic preference from a demographic label.
